data.description:
## Context / Problem

Two backend-neutral core modules load Jira rich-text/limit modules by literal lazy-load key
and run them unconditionally — for ANY configured backend:

- `outbound_comments.py:68` (`_ADF_KEY = "rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.adf"`, loaded at
  `:75-85`) and `outbound_comments.py:71` (`_COMMENT_LIMITS_KEY =
  "rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.comment_limits"`, loaded at `:88`) — comment-body
  formatting/truncation on the outbound comment-diff path.
- `inbound_translate.py:315` (`_ADF_KEY_APPLIER`, loaded at `:319-324`) —
  `_normalize_adf_body` decodes a raw ADF dict to text as defense-in-depth on the inbound
  apply path (bug 1bb2-5da5).

A GitHub/Linear backend would still have its comments ADF-encoded and Jira-limit-truncated.
These literal keys are the last vendor reach-through in neutral core paths after the sibling
stories land; epic bbf1's separation goal ("a second backend drops in behind the port")
requires them behind the port.

## Approach

Route both modules through the port roles that already own these responsibilities:

- `FieldSanitizer` (already pinned in `_backend.py` as the vendor hard-limit defense, with
  `sanitize_comment(body: str) -> str`) gains the comment-fit surface the outbound comment
  path actually needs (fit-to-limit + marker-preserving truncation semantics currently
  provided by `adapters/jira/comment_limits.py` and `adapters/jira/adf.py`).
  `outbound_comments.py` resolves the configured backend's sanitizer via the registry seam
  (`select_backend(load_config())` — pattern proven at `outbound_differ.py:450-454`) instead
  of lazy-loading vendor modules, and the two literal keys at `outbound_comments.py:68,71`
  are deleted.
- `InboundMapper` gains `normalize_rich_text(body: Any) -> str` (Jira: `adf_to_text` for
  dicts, identity for strings — exactly today's `_normalize_adf_body`,
  `inbound_translate.py:349+`). `inbound_translate.py` calls the backend's inbound mapper;
  the literal key at `inbound_translate.py:315` is deleted. The defense-in-depth property
  (a raw vendor rich-text dict on the wire never reaches an EDIT event's `description`) is
  preserved backend-neutrally.
- Add a sweep case to `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py`: no
  package-root core module contains the literal string `"rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira"`,
  with a single recorded exemption for `inbound_fields.py:24` — that module IS the Jira
  `InboundMapper` implementation (delegated by `adapters/jira/backend.py:43-47`) that
  remains at the package root only for loader location-pinning reasons; its physical
  relocation is out of this epic's scope and tracked by follow-up ticket
  `556a-5a1f-adb3-4139` (linked `discovered_from` epic bbf1).

Rejected alternatives: (a) a new sixth role Protocol ("RichTextCodec") — YAGNI: FieldSanitizer
already owns vendor-limit defense per its `_backend.py` docstring and InboundMapper already
owns vendor→local payload translation; fewer than 3 call sites for a new role. (b) Leaving
the lazy keys because "they are only strings, not imports" — the loader still executes vendor
code on neutral paths for any backend, which is the substance of the leak.

## Scope

- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_backend.py (FieldSanitizer comment-fit surface; InboundMapper.normalize_rich_text)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/outbound_comments.py (route via sanitizer; delete both literal keys)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py (route via inbound mapper; delete the literal key)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/backend.py (implement the new members, delegating to adf/comment_limits)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_fields.py (Jira mapper: normalize_rich_text implementation)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py (literal-key sweep with the recorded inbound_fields.py exemption)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (comment formatting/truncation + normalize_rich_text behavior tests)

## Testing

- Happy path: an outbound comment body formats and truncates byte-identically to today via
  the sanitizer route; a plain-string inbound description passes through
  `normalize_rich_text` unchanged.
- Failure/edge: an over-limit comment truncates with today's marker semantics; a raw ADF dict
  arriving on the inbound apply path is decoded to text and never written verbatim into an
  EDIT event (the bug 1bb2-5da5 regression case, now backend-neutral).
- Gate: the sweep case fails when any package-root core module (except the recorded
  `inbound_fields.py` exemption) contains the `"rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira"` literal.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `outbound_comments.py` and `inbound_translate.py` contain neither `adapters.jira`
      imports nor `"rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira"` lazy-key literals — verified by the new
      sweep case in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py`.
- [ ] Comment formatting/truncation and inbound rich-text normalization behave identically
      for Jira — verified by the behavior tests in Testing (including the 1bb2-5da5
      regression case).
- [ ] The sweep gate documents the single `inbound_fields.py` exemption with its rationale
      and the follow-up ticket id — verified by reading the gate test.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green; whole test tree collects with 0 errors.
data.title: Rich-text codec and comment limits behind the port: delete the adapters.jira lazy-key literals from core
